segments.text: That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But when the fulness of the time was come,
God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
And because ye are sons,
God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free
segments.speaker: Paul
